name: Guntur Sannam (S4 / S17)
color: Bright to deep red; high ASTA
uses: Curries, chutneys, marinades, spice blends, hot sauces, oleoresin
why: Balanced heat + color + aroma; export-grade demand.
drying: Sun-dried on open yards for 10–20 days; hand-turned; no artificial heat.
story: There is a particular quality to the Guntur sky in November — a hard, relentless brightness over miles of red drying yards.

name: Teja (S17 Teja)
uses: Industrial spice, calibrated heat blends, hot sauces, extracts
why: Preferred when heat must be measurable and consistent.
drying: Open-yard drying; monitored to avoid cracking.
story: Teja is heat with discipline — it builds, lingers, and communicates.

name: Byadgi (AP Traded)
color: Deep red-maroon; very high color value
uses: Color-forward gravies, Chettinad/Coorg styles, natural color extracts
why: Extraordinary color yield; premium for natural reds.
drying: Careful drying to preserve maroon color; partial shade in afternoons.
story: The painter’s chilli — used when the dish must look magnificent without overwhelming heat.

name: 334 Variety
color: Medium to bright red; smooth skin
drying: Open-yard sun drying 12–18 days; surfaces cleaned carefully.
story: Dependability made into a chilli — consistent season after season.

name: Warangal Chilli
uses: Telangana curries, lentils; commercial blends needing body + heat
drying: Raised mats; low humidity + strong sun; 15–20 days.
story: Unmistakably Telangana — fierce, proud, and direct.

name: Khammam Region Chilli
why: Fuller pods; richer flavor and stronger color.
story: A crossroads chilli — blending commercial scale and local character.

name: Ramnad Mundu
uses: Chettinad cuisine, fish curries, temple foods
why: Iconic coastal variety with intense character.
drying: Woven mats; sea breeze + sun; 15–25 days.
story: Small in size, massive in legacy — the heat of the Coromandel sky inside a round pod.

name: Tamil Nadu Local Sun-Dried Varieties
why: Heritage diversity with deep flavor complexity.
drying: Terraces/platforms; turned daily; protected from humidity.
story: A living feedback loop between farm and kitchen — regional diversity you can taste.

name: Byadgi Dabbi
color: Deep maroon; exceptional natural red pigment
uses: Color-forward gravies, marinades, natural color extracts
why: Global gold standard for clean-label red color.
drying: Shade-assisted drying to preserve deep maroon.
story: A heritage chilli powering the global shift to natural colors — without losing its roots.

name: Byadgi Kaddi
uses: Powders, spice mixes; tempering for rapid color release
drying: Elevated mats for airflow; prevent over-drying.
story: A few pods in hot oil, and everything turns jewel-red — instantly.
